# Transcrição: 4. My Rules of the Phone

- **Fonte:** 4. My Rules of the Phone
- **Duração:** 03:54
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, anyone that has ever worked with me or seen me work this little device will tell you the following things about me. These have become the disciplines I use, the rules if you will, to ensure my success with a telephone. You play by these rules, I guarantee you, guarantee you success with a phone and guarantee you success in your business. Number one, always make a call with a purpose and full attention to that call. Never ever make a call without true purpose. This salespeople call just to make a call and never without full purpose and complete attention on that call. Number two, you want to always have multiple reasons if that initial purpose doesn't satisfy you. We'll talk about that. I need to have different places to go to keep interest with a caller but with one main purpose in mind. Three, when I sit down to make calls, I never ever make one call. This is a major mistake. People sit down and make one call and then go have a cup of coffee. I'll sit down and block out 40 minutes and see how many calls I can make in 40 minutes without ever putting a phone back into the cradle or literally just keep this phone in my hand and just keep calling the next place. Number four, always, always, always leave a message. 98% of all salespeople break that rule. And I'll show you how to leave a message, why to leave a message, and how to leave multiple messages successfully. Number five, always talk to multiple people. I never call one person. Hey, John's not in right now. No problem. Hey, can I leave a message for John? Can I go to his voicemail? I leave a message for John and go back to zero and talk to somebody below John. I want to talk to everybody. I want to multiply my presence. I want to put my footprint on planet Earth, if you will, with my phone calls. Number six, always try another way to get that person. If somebody's not in, I want to figure out another way to contact him. Emails, texting, another number. Where would they be? Where could I find them? I'm extremely aggressive on the phone, okay, by trying to find another way to speak to this person. Seven, always go for a cell number. Now, this could change over time, depending on what technology does. It used to be always go for an office number. It used to be before that go for the home. Then it was the office, okay? Today it's look, cell numbers. You want cell numbers, why? Because I can text content. I can text information. I can text video. I can text full packages to a phone that sits in the palm of the buyer's hand. Many people are getting rid of their home phones and replacing them with this, so I want that cell phone. Number eight, always collect email addresses. Okay, again, a piece of technology that could change. Always collect an email address in these conversations. Nine, always follow up a phone call with another type of communication. I don't do one of these without contacting another person. Or using another terminal or another mode of communication to follow someone up. So if I do a phone call, I might follow it up with a text. I might follow it up with an email. I might follow it up with a mail.

## [03:00 - 03:54]

I might follow it up with a personal visit. You with me? Same thing with the reverse. If I sent mail out, if I sent an email out, if I sent a text, I want to follow it up with a phone call. Again, multiple. I think in terms of military combat, I want to bring all my forces. I want to move everything in, okay? Not just the one tool, weapon, or soldiers that I depend on. Ten, you want to approach every call with a great attitude. Every call needs to be approached with, hey, I have a great attitude and I have a successful outcome in my mind. I don't make a call with the intention of making a call. I make the call with the intention of a successful outcome. I'm going to get something out of this call, whether it's create a great impression, get an appointment, get an opportunity, send a package. One successful outcome that will make a difference in that customer and in my career. Learn the rules, play by the rules, and you'll win.
